trigger.reason: Google DeepMind released Gemini-powered smart home speaker ($99.99) — a new product with broad market attention targeting the smart home/IoT vertical, likely to trigger sympathy-fade selling in adjacent consumer-tech and IoT stocks.

primary_event.headline: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
primary_event.summary: Google released a new $99.99 Google Home Speaker powered by generative AI (Gemini) that replaces rigid voice commands with conversational interactions. This product launch signals Google's aggressive push into AI-powered consumer hardware and smart home devices.

secondary_events.headline: Pinterest launches an experimental AI shopping app called 'Ask Pinterest'

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed Google DeepMind's Gemini-powered $99.99 smart home speaker launch and its sympathy-fade impact on AI-adjacent software/IoT names.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger is a consumer smart-home hardware release targeting IoT/voice-assistant verticals — but the curated basket is mostly enterprise vertical-workflow SaaS (HR/payroll, banking, government, nonprofit, pharmacy automation) with no real exposure to a Google Home speaker. Most candidates fell on broad AI-sympathy fear despite their moats being regulatory, physical, or workflow-embedded — classic false-victim setup. AUDC (voice telephony) is excluded as a real-victim control with no filings; NCNO is up and skipped.

discoveries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors mention AI as a peripheral competitive concern (self-driving vehicles, AI-enabled competing tools) but core business is automated photo enforcement under multi-year municipal contracts (NYCDOT 5-year contract effective Jan 2026, ~17.9% of revenue) requiring physical cameras and court-admissible evidentiary chains.
discoveries.thesis: A Google Home consumer speaker has zero overlap with municipal photo-enforcement contracts, court-admissible chain of custody, or the physical camera infrastructure VRRM operates. The sharp drop on light volume looks like indiscriminate AI-basket selling.
discoveries.what_confirms: Reversal within days with no follow-through news; NYCDOT contract execution proceeding per plan; no municipal customer attrition signals.
discoveries.what_kills: News of a municipality piloting AI-only enforcement (no physical cameras); a key Commercial Services customer non-renewal (the 10-Q flagged one significant CS customer in negotiation); Ontario-style legislative bans spreading.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Physical-infrastructure + municipal-contract names get swept into AI sympathy fades despite having essentially zero capability overlap with generative AI products.
discoveries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: VRRM is in the broad 'AI-adjacent tech' basket and dropped meaningfully on a low-volume day with no company-specific news — pure thematic blast radius.
research_pointers: Track NYCDOT new-contract execution milestones
Identify the Commercial Services customer in renewal negotiation (10% of revenue)
Monitor for additional Canadian/US jurisdictions banning automated enforcement

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss generative/agentic AI primarily as an internal operational risk and adoption risk in regulated industries, not as a replacement for WMS. Core business is enterprise supply-chain cloud subscriptions with 9-12+ month sales cycles and deep integration with customer ERP/warehouse hardware.
discoveries.thesis: Google Home speakers don't compete with enterprise warehouse management systems. MANH's moat is multi-year deployments integrating with physical warehouse hardware and ERP systems — switching cost is the moat. Already extended over five days, suggesting broad SaaS de-rating rather than name-specific impairment.
discoveries.what_confirms: No customer churn in upcoming quarter; continued WMS deal flow; competitors like Blue Yonder not deploying disruptive AI-native WMS.
discoveries.what_kills: A hyperscaler announcing an AI-native WMS replacement; a Tier-1 retailer publicly migrating off MANH to an LLM-orchestrated supply chain platform; further extension of the 5-day decline on rising volume.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Vertical enterprise SaaS with deep integration moats gets repriced on every AI consumer release regardless of business-model overlap — five-day extension warrants caution on entry timing.
discoveries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: MANH is a high-multiple vertical SaaS name in the AI-adjacent basket, dropping on no company-specific news on a Gemini consumer launch day.
research_pointers: Check competitor (Blue Yonder, SAP, Oracle) WMS announcements for agentic AI features
Monitor cloud subscription bookings trend in next earnings
Watch for retail customer concentration shifts given retail sector disruption

discoveries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors emphasize Blackbaud actively deploying agentic AI as a competitive enhancement and acknowledge AI-driven sentiment risk to stock price — but core business is multi-year subscription contracts (40% renewing 2026, 30% in 2027, 30% in 2028) with nonprofit-specific workflows, tax-exempt compliance, and donor data spanning 20+ years per customer.
discoveries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker doesn't replace nonprofit fundraising / donor management workflows with embedded payment processing and 20+ years of historical donor data. Blackbaud's own filings explicitly warn that AI sentiment can drive stock volatility independent of fundamentals — this is that pattern.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable renewal rates in next earnings; continued Integrated Payments transaction volume growth; no nonprofit customer migration to general-purpose AI tools.
discoveries.what_kills: A nonprofit-focused AI startup gaining material traction with enterprise nonprofits; renewal-rate compression in upcoming quarter; new Security Incident developments.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Vertical SaaS with switching cost from decades of customer data is the canonical false-victim pattern on AI sympathy days — even more so when the company's own Risk Factors flag the sentiment risk explicitly.
discoveries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: BLKB is a vertical nonprofit SaaS in the AI-adjacent basket, falling on no company news on a Google consumer hardware day.
research_pointers: Track 2026 renewal cohort outcomes (40% of contracts)
Monitor charitable giving trends for macro headwinds
Watch for any new Security Incident regulatory updates

discoveries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors explicitly flag that AI/automation may 'reduce our clients' need for existing or future employees who are or would be potential users of our solution' — a real per-employee revenue exposure to workforce displacement. Also acknowledges AI may render existing HCM solutions obsolete. However, payroll tax filing, state UI, jurisdictional compliance remain the moat.
discoveries.thesis: A consumer Gemini speaker has no overlap with payroll tax compliance, but PAYC's filings do reveal a real second-order AI risk (employee headcount compression at customers). Move is partial — five-day decline already extended. The Google Home trigger specifically is not the right driver; this is broader AI-on-HR-software anxiety.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable client employee counts in next quarter; continued cross-sell success on existing modules.
discoveries.what_kills: Material guide-down on per-employee pricing or client headcount; a major HR-AI agent launch from Microsoft/Google specifically targeting SMB payroll; further breakdown of the already-extended 5-day chart.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Vertical HR/payroll SaaS has a real but second-order AI risk (customers shedding workers reduces per-seat revenue) — not zero exposure, but the panic on a Google smart-speaker day is mis-attributed.
discoveries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PAYC is in the vertical-workflow HR basket and fell on no company news on volume — pure thematic fade.
research_pointers: Watch client employee count trends as proxy for AI-driven workforce compression
Compare PAYC pricing power vs. PCTY, ADP in upcoming quarters
Monitor any Microsoft Copilot/Gemini agent product targeting HR back-office

discoveries.name: Paylocity Holding Corporation
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors note that 'New technologies that involve AI or machine learning... may emerge that are able to deliver solutions at lower prices, more efficiently or more conveniently than our solutions' — generalized AI competitive risk language without specific exposure to consumer voice assistants. Core moat: ACH banking relationships, payroll tax filing, jurisdiction-specific compliance.
discoveries.thesis: Same logic as PAYC — Google Home doesn't file 941s or handle state UI registrations. However the move is on top of an already-extended 5-day decline, suggesting the broader HR/payroll AI fear is in motion. Sympathy fade is mis-targeted to this specific Gemini trigger.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable client retention; healthy bookings in upcoming Q1 (peak season just behind).
discoveries.what_kills: An AI-native payroll competitor gaining material SMB traction; client headcount compression; further leg down on rising volume.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a name is already down ~10% over five days and adds another sympathy-fade leg on a thematically unrelated trigger, the entry timing matters — the panic may have legs even if this specific trigger is wrong.
discoveries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PCTY in vertical HR-SaaS basket, falling on no company news during a Google consumer hardware event.
research_pointers: Check PCTY vs. PAYC relative bookings trend
Monitor SMB payroll AI startup funding/launches
Wait for 5-day decline to stabilize before sizing

discoveries.name: OneSpan Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors mention AI lowering barriers to entry and competitors integrating GenAI faster, but core business is regulated authentication for banks (FIDO2/passkey standards-bound), Digipass hardware tokens, and electronic signatures. Standards bodies, not AI, define the product category.
discoveries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker doesn't authenticate banking customers or sign legal documents. OSPN's regulated authentication and e-signature business is bound to PSD2/eIDAS standards. The move is small but on lighter volume, classic indiscriminate basket selling.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued Nok Nok integration progress; Build38 close on schedule (March 2026); banking customer retention.
discoveries.what_kills: A major bank publicly choosing an AI-native authentication vendor over OSPN; FIDO Alliance changes that disadvantage OSPN.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Standards-bound regulated software (auth, compliance) is structurally insulated from AI consumer product launches — high-confidence false victim setup.
discoveries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: OSPN small-cap cyber/auth name in AI-adjacent basket, drifting lower on light volume.
research_pointers: Track Build38 acquisition close timing
Monitor Digipass hardware revenue trajectory vs. software mix
Watch FIDO2/passkey adoption rate at top bank customers

discoveries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors explicitly flag GenAI as a competitive threat: 'a new product could gain rapid scale at the expense of existing brands through harnessing a new technology (such as generative artificial intelligence)' and translation services may reduce general interest in language learning. However DUOL has launched Duolingo Max using GenAI and the moat is gamification/streak loops.
discoveries.thesis: The Google Home speaker trigger is unrelated to language learning, but DUOL has real (though manageable) exposure to consumer AI products. The dopamine-loop/streak moat is the defense, and DUOL is monetizing AI via Duolingo Max. Move on light volume looks more like consumer-AI basket fade than fundamental threat.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued DAU growth and Max subscription uptake in next quarter; stable paid conversion.
discoveries.what_kills: A flagship consumer AI tutor product (ChatGPT Tutor, Gemini Tutor) gaining material DAU traction; declining DAUs or streak metrics; mobile platform fee or policy changes from Apple/Google.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Consumer-AI-exposed names should not be classified as false victims on every AI launch — they have real exposure but the question is whether the trigger is the right one. A smart-speaker launch is the wrong driver for an edtech fade.
discoveries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: DUOL is the canonical consumer-AI-exposed name and gets swept up in any Google/OpenAI consumer release as basket fade.
research_pointers: Track Duolingo Max conversion and ARPU uplift
Monitor DAU/MAU trend post-Google launches historically
Watch Apple/Google app store policy changes

discoveries.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss AI primarily as a cybersecurity threat and a competitive challenge requiring TYL to adopt AI features, with explicit acknowledgement that 'delays in our adoption or innovation could render our offerings less competitive.' Core business is government procurement with year-long sales cycles and decades-long municipal contracts.
discoveries.thesis: No government CIO is replacing court records of record with a Google Home speaker. TYL's moat is FedRAMP/state-specific compliance and entrenched multi-decade municipal relationships. The move on very light volume (0.65x) is classic indiscriminate AI fade.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued cloud migration of legacy government customers; stable bookings; convertible notes settled in cash as planned.
discoveries.what_kills: A government-specific AI-native competitor winning a flagship state contract; FedRAMP changes that advantage AI-native vendors.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Government vertical SaaS is the deepest moat in software (procurement cycles, compliance, decades of contracts) — false victim pattern is extremely reliable here.
discoveries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: TYL is in the vertical government-SaaS basket and fell on very low volume with no company news.
research_pointers: Track 2026 Convertible Notes maturity settlement (March 15, 2026)
Monitor government cloud transition bookings
Watch for any RFP losses to AI-native competitors

discoveries.name: Omnicell, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss AI as a peripheral technology evolution that may produce competing solutions and an internal cybersecurity threat, but core business is physical pharmacy automation robotics in hospitals with DEA Schedule II compliance, bar-code dispensing, and physical narcotic cabinets.
discoveries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker cannot unlock a narcotic cabinet or dispense Schedule II controlled substances. OMCL's physical robotics moat in hospital pharmacies is essentially zero overlap with consumer AI. Move on average volume but no company news — pure thematic fade.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable booking trends in central pharmacy automation; continued SaaS/Expert Services renewals.
discoveries.what_kills: Hospital customer capital budget compression; a major customer cancellation; deterioration in SaaS retention.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Healthcare vertical with physical hardware + DEA compliance is essentially immune to consumer AI launches — the basket-selling completely ignores the physical-world moat.
discoveries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: OMCL is a healthcare tech name in the broader AI-adjacent basket, falling on no company news.
research_pointers: Track central pharmacy automation deal flow
Monitor SaaS/Expert Services subscription renewal rates
Watch hospital capital spending environment given tariff/budget pressures

skipped.reason: Stock is UP today (+4.24%) — no panic to fade; sympathy-fade screen requires negative move.
Filings entirely unavailable (both 10-K and 10-Q missing); also flagged as real_victim_control where AI voice agent panic is plausibly correct — cannot make a defensible call without filings.

_trigger.headline: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
_trigger.summary: Google released a new $99.99 Google Home Speaker powered by generative AI (Gemini) that replaces rigid voice commands with conversational interactions. This product launch signals Google's aggressive push into AI-powered consumer hardware and smart home devices.
